friction_items.description: A fresh AI session sees 344 MCP tools at startup. Triage cost is enormous: scanning even the short descriptions burns several thousand context tokens before the first useful call. tool_route is the prescribed entrypoint but it is one entry among 344, with no `priority` flag in the schema. A naive AI without prior Research-OS memory will read several wrong-namespace tools before landing on tool_route. The architecture assumes the AI already knows to call sys_boot + tool_route first, but the tool list itself does not enforce or signal this ordering.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Expose a curated `_router` subset (sys_boot, tool_route, sys_help, sys_active_tools, sys_protocol_get, tool_plan_advance) as the only tools surfaced on first list_tools, with the remaining 338 reachable via sys_semantic_tool_search. Or annotate every non-router tool with `boot_visible: false` so MCP clients can filter.

friction_items.description: The server ships BOTH the unified dispatchers AND the legacy split tools simultaneously: tool_plan + tool_plan_{turn,advance,clear}; sys_path + sys_path_{create,abandon,list}; tool_ground + tool_grounding_register + tool_ground_from_context; tool_search + tool_search_{semantic_scholar,pubmed,crossref,arxiv,web}; tool_verify + tool_claim_verify + tool_grounding_verify; tool_lessons + tool_lessons_{record,consult}; mem_log + mem_methods_append + mem_decision_log + mem_hypothesis_update + mem_analysis_log. A naive AI cannot tell from the schema which is canonical and will mix old + new names within a single session, leaving an inconsistent audit trail. Documentation telling the AI to prefer the unified entry lives only inside the long `description` field — easy to miss when triaging.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Hide the legacy split tools from list_tools by default once the unified dispatcher exists (set deprecated=true + filter unless researcher_config.show_deprecated=true). Keep the handler aliases so existing integrations still work, but stop advertising them. Saves ~25 tools from the visible surface.

friction_items.description: For the prompt 'I have 8 interview transcripts, let's code them for themes' tool_route picks ONE protocol from at least 8 plausible matches: methodology/qualitative_research (core, umbrella), methodology/qualitative_pii_redaction (core, prerequisite), methodology/qualitative_quality_audit (core, late gate), methodology/coding_scheme_development (core), methodology/inter_rater_reliability (core), qualitative/method/thematic_analysis_braun_clarke (pack), qualitative/method/grounded_theory_iteration (pack), qualitative/coding/coding_scheme_iteration (pack). A naive AI receiving a single primary_protocol from tool_route will not know there are 7 other contenders unless it also calls tool_semantic_route. The pack vs core distinction is invisible in the route result and the relationships among the 8 (umbrella vs branch vs prerequisite vs late audit) are not surfaced as a graph.
friction_items.suggested_fix: tool_route should return primary + 2-3 alternatives + a one-line rationale (`semantic confidence 0.82 over 0.81 — close call, consider also: ...`). Surface a protocol_graph endpoint that returns the dependency edges (qualitative_pii_redaction → qualitative_research → thematic_analysis_braun_clarke → member_checking → qualitative_report_format → qualitative_quality_audit) so the AI can see the pipeline shape in one call.

friction_items.description: qualitative_research declares a HARD GATE: if inputs/raw_data/ has transcripts and inputs/raw_data_redacted/ does not, the protocol routes to qualitative_pii_redaction. A naive AI walking qualitative_research step-by-step will hit this in step `ingest_transcripts`, halfway through. The gate fire message is buried in step description prose ('STOP and route to methodology/qualitative_pii_redaction first') and not surfaced as a tool-result error code. The AI may interpret it as documentation rather than a blocking instruction and proceed to coding anyway.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Move the prerequisite check into sys_protocol_get's response when format='summary' — return prerequisite_status: {satisfied: false, missing: ['raw_data_redacted/'], next_protocol: 'methodology/qualitative_pii_redaction'} so the AI sees it before walking steps. Have tool_route detect transcripts in inputs/raw_data/ at routing time and prepend qualitative_pii_redaction to the decomposition automatically.

friction_items.description: qualitative_pii_redaction's actual redaction work (presidio + spaCy NER + regex) is not implemented as an MCP tool. The protocol instructs the AI to invoke tool_python_exec with a script the AI must compose. A naive AI without Microsoft Presidio knowledge will spend turns writing a brittle pipeline, fail with `en_core_web_lg not installed`, and either give up or hand off to the researcher. There is no tool_qualitative_redact_transcripts MCP tool to encapsulate this.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a first-class tool_qualitative_pii_redact MCP tool (in src/research_os_qualitative/tools.py) that takes a transcript path + pii_policy.md and writes the redacted output + ledger deterministically. Protocol step becomes one tool call, not a script-authoring exercise.

friction_items.description: Multiple protocols (qualitative_research, qualitative_pii_redaction) instruct the AI to write `workspace/<step>/step_summary.yaml` with fields like step_intent, figure_required, table_required, literature_required. These fields gate the completeness audit downstream. A naive AI without prior Research-OS exposure will not know this YAML exists, will skip it, then later hit a `tool_audit_step_completeness` BLOCK on 'missing required figure' or 'literature_required: true but no literature loop run'. There is no tool that creates this file from the protocol's expected_outputs — the AI must hand-author it.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Auto-generate step_summary.yaml inside sys_path operation='create' by reading the routed protocol's expected_outputs + quality_bar. The AI never touches it unless it wants to override. Or expose tool_step_intent_set as a one-call helper.

friction_items.description: When a quality gate fires (tool_plan_advance returns status='blocked', or tool_audit_synthesis returns blockers), the message references internal field names ('per_code_kappa_minimum: 0.70 not met', 'coverage_floor_for_submission: 0.80'). A naive AI sees these without context — there is no `recovery_recipe` field linking the blocker to the protocol step that produces the missing artefact. The AI either calls override_gate=true (defeating the purpose of the audit) or thrashes between sys_protocol_get and the failing tool.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Every gate/blocker response should include {failing_field, plain_english, fix_step_id, fix_protocol_name, fix_tool_call_example}. Make the recovery path a single field the AI can read and act on, not text the AI must parse out of a description string.

friction_items.description: tool_qualitative_quote_provenance registers ONE quote per call. For an 8-participant study with ~5 themes and 3 quotes per theme that is ~15 sequential tool calls just for registry maintenance. A naive AI will skip this (because the protocol doesn't explicitly enumerate every required call) and then fail the qualitative_report_format audit that consumes the registry. The shape of the API forces an N+1 pattern.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add bulk mode: tool_qualitative_quote_provenance(mode='bulk', entries=[{quote, participant_id, ...}, ...]). Or have tool_qualitative_quote_provenance accept a path to a quotes.csv / quotes.jsonl file. Better still, have the report-format audit auto-extract [P01:42-47]-style markers from the manuscript and register them on the AI's behalf.

friction_items.description: Several routing-tier tool descriptions exceed 700 characters (tool_route: 750+, sys_protocol_get: 820+, sys_protocol_list: 820+, tool_protocols_list: 600+, sys_help: 650+). A naive AI loading list_tools spends a lot of context on routing-tool descriptions before reaching domain tools. The `short` field exists for some tools but not all, and the MCP wire format ships `description` not `short`.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Cap MCP-shipped `description` at 200 chars (one sentence: what it does, when to call it). Move the detailed prose into `sys_tool_describe` which the AI calls only on demand. Saves ~15-25K tokens at boot.

friction_items.description: The three qualitative pack tools (tool_qualitative_codebook_diff, tool_qualitative_quote_provenance, tool_qualitative_select_standard) are registered via the plugin registry, not in server.py's TOOL_DEFINITIONS. They DO surface in list_tools at runtime, but their pack provenance ('this came from the qualitative pack') is invisible in the tool schema. A naive AI cannot tell what is core vs pack contribution, which matters for understanding why a tool is or is not available in a given project.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a `pack: qualitative` annotation to every register_tool call (and `pack: core` to TOOL_DEFINITIONS). Surface it in sys_tool_describe output. tool_tools_list already takes a scope filter — make it the default sort key in the listing.

friction_items.description: The router_entries.py decomposition (e.g. qualitative_report_format: [protocol, tool_qualitative_select_standard, tool_qualitative_quote_provenance]) does not exactly match the YAML steps (which reference tool_search, mem_log, sys_file_list and more). A naive AI following the decomposition will call only the listed tools; one following the protocol YAML will call others. The two sources of truth drift.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Generate the decomposition AT LOAD TIME by parsing the protocol YAML's `description` blocks for tool references (regex on `tool_\w+` / `sys_\w+` / `mem_\w+`). Single source of truth = the YAML; the router entry only provides the trigger phrases + intent_class.

friction_items.description: tool_qualitative_select_standard with standard='auto' reads workspace/study_design.yaml. No protocol creates that file. The 'auto' default silently falls back to SRQR with a reason string ('study_design.yaml not found; defaulting to SRQR') instead of escalating to ask_user. A naive AI will accept the wrong standard and burn turns later when the COREQ-specific items don't match the manuscript.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either (a) have qualitative_research.design_protocol emit a minimal study_design.yaml as a deliverable, or (b) have tool_qualitative_select_standard return status='ask_user' when the design file is missing AND the caller passed 'auto', with a recommended next call (the AI surfaces this to the researcher rather than picking blind).

friction_items.description: qualitative_quality_audit's saturation_evidence_check expects a flat saturation curve. With N=8 the curve is statistically noisy and the binary verdict (SATURATED vs NOT_SATURATED) is dubious. A naive AI gets a hard BLOCK on a methodologically defensible small study and has no path to a 'limitation documented' branch except by reading prose and calling override_gate.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Three-valued verdict (SATURATED | LIKELY_NOT_SATURATED | UNDERPOWERED_TO_JUDGE) — return UNDERPOWERED_TO_JUDGE when N < 12 AND a small_n_limitations.md exists, and treat that as a documented limitation rather than a blocker.

friction_items.description: tool_qualitative_codebook_diff hardcodes the output filename to 'diff_v1_to_v2.md' regardless of the actual input version numbers. Diffing v3 → v4 overwrites the v1 → v2 diff silently. A naive AI iterating through rounds will lose its history and not know why.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Parse the version numbers from the codebook filenames (e.g. codebook_v3.yaml → v=3) and name the output diff_v{N}_to_v{M}.md, matching the docstring claim. Or accept explicit v1_label / v2_label args.

friction_items.description: When tool_route selects qualitative/method/thematic_analysis_braun_clarke it does not annotate the result with `source: pack:qualitative` vs `source: core`. A naive AI reading the result cannot tell whether the chosen protocol is a core methodology or a pack contribution, which matters when reasoning about availability across projects.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add `source` (core|pack:<name>) and `protocol_kind` (umbrella|method|audit|prerequisite|report) fields to every tool_route result.

convoluted_tools: tool_plan (vs tool_plan_advance vs tool_plan_turn vs tool_plan_clear)
sys_path (vs sys_path_create vs sys_path_abandon vs sys_path_list)
mem_log (vs mem_methods_append vs mem_decision_log vs mem_hypothesis_update vs mem_analysis_log vs mem_analysis_log)
sys_protocol_get (5 format modes overload one tool)
confusing_protocols: methodology/qualitative_research vs qualitative/method/thematic_analysis_braun_clarke (umbrella vs method, overlap is invisible)
methodology/coding_scheme_development vs qualitative/coding/coding_scheme_iteration (one is pre-coding setup, the other is multi-round refinement — naive AI cannot tell from triggers)
methodology/qualitative_quality_audit vs qualitative/output/qualitative_report_format (one is a late audit gate, the other is the report-format checklist; both reference COREQ/SRQR)
methodology/qualitative_pii_redaction (prerequisite gate that is not obviously a prerequisite from the tool surface)
qualitative/method/grounded_theory_iteration vs qualitative/coding/coding_scheme_iteration (both talk about iteration; one is GT-branch-specific, the other is generic)
recommendations: Phase 9 consolidation: hide deprecated split tools (tool_plan_*, sys_path_*, tool_grounding_*, tool_search_*, mem_methods_append family) from list_tools by default. Keep aliases live but invisible. Cuts ~25 tools from the boot surface.

Add `pack` annotation (core | qualitative | humanities | theory_math | wet_lab | engineering | slurm | synapse | cytoscape | nextflow | redcap | snakemake) to every tool and protocol, and surface it in tool_route, sys_active_tools, and tool_tools_list output by default.

Add a tool_qualitative_pii_redact MCP tool that wraps presidio + spaCy + regex behind a single call. Today the protocol punts to tool_python_exec with a script the AI must author from scratch — that is the single highest friction point in the whole scenario.
Auto-generate step_summary.yaml inside sys_path operation='create' from the routed protocol's expected_outputs + quality_bar. Remove the invisible-contract pitfall entirely.

tool_route should return primary + 2-3 alternatives + the pack provenance + a one-line rationale. With 8 qualitative protocols overlapping on 'coding'/'qualitative' triggers, picking one without surfacing the others guarantees a wrong-protocol session at least 30% of the time.

Add bulk mode to tool_qualitative_quote_provenance OR auto-extract [P01:42-47] markers from the manuscript. Today the per-quote API forces ~15 sequential calls for a typical 5-theme report, which a naive AI will skip.

Every gate/blocker response must carry a `recovery_recipe` structured field (failing_field, plain_english, fix_step_id, fix_protocol_name, fix_tool_call_example). Make recovery a single read, not a description-parsing exercise.
Cap MCP-shipped tool `description` at ~200 chars. Move detail to sys_tool_describe. Saves ~15-25K tokens on first list_tools call.

Three-valued saturation verdict (SATURATED | LIKELY_NOT_SATURATED | UNDERPOWERED_TO_JUDGE) for the qualitative_quality_audit. With N=8 the binary verdict is methodologically dubious and forces override_gate use.

Surface a /protocol_graph endpoint (or a sys_protocol_graph tool) that returns the dependency edges among qualitative_pii_redaction → qualitative_research → thematic_analysis_braun_clarke → member_checking → qualitative_report_format → qualitative_quality_audit. Lets the AI see the pipeline shape in one call instead of inferring it from prose.
